proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
Patients receiving treatment_sotorasib have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_sotorasib.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
Patients receiving treatment_osimertinib have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_osimertinib.

analyses.result_summary: Pembrolizumab-treated patients (n=25008) had ORR 0.1739 vs 0.1640 in untreated (n=24992). Absolute difference +0.0099 (chi-square p=0.0034). Small but statistically significant marginal benefit.
Sotorasib-treated patients (n=17544) had ORR 0.1695 vs 0.1687 in untreated. Difference +0.0008, chi-square p=0.829. No detectable marginal effect.
Olaparib-treated patients (n=14999) had ORR 0.1699 vs 0.1685 in untreated. Difference +0.0013, chi-square p=0.724. No detectable marginal effect.
Osimertinib-treated patients (n=14976) had ORR 0.1655 vs 0.1704 in untreated. Difference -0.0050, chi-square p=0.179. No detectable marginal effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_osimertinib increases objective_response specifically among patients with egfr_mutation=1 (positive treatment x biomarker interaction on the log-odds scale).
treatment_sotorasib increases objective_response specifically among patients with kras_g12c=1 (positive interaction).
treatment_olaparib increases objective_response specifically among patients with brca2_mutation=1 (positive interaction).
treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response specifically among patients with tmb_high=1 (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Among EGFR-mutant patients (n=6517), ORR was 0.164 on osimertinib vs 0.166 off (p=0.87). Interaction term log-OR=+0.024, p=0.758. No EGFR-targeted effect detected.
Among KRAS G12C-positive patients (n=6368), ORR was 0.175 on sotorasib vs 0.166 off (p=0.43). Interaction log-OR=+0.059, p=0.427. No KRAS-targeted effect detected.
Among BRCA2-mutant patients (n=1519), ORR was 0.146 on olaparib vs 0.168 off (p=0.346). Interaction log-OR=-0.177, p=0.275. Direction is opposite to expected and not significant.

Among tmb_high=1 patients (n=14258), ORR was 0.197 on pembrolizumab vs 0.167 off (diff +0.0295, p=5.4e-6). Among tmb_high=0 patients no effect (diff +0.002, p=0.61). Interaction log-OR=+0.184, p=4.2e-4. Strong and confirmatory.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with higher objective_response across all patients (positive main effect).
The benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response increases with higher pdl1_tps (positive interaction).
Among patients with pdl1_tps >= 0.5 ('PD-L1 high'), treatment_pembrolizumab raises objective_response, while in pdl1_tps < 0.5 patients it does not.

analyses.result_summary: pdl1_tps (range 0-0.80) had log-OR=+0.166, p=0.0032 for ORR. Modest positive main effect, but mostly driven by pembrolizumab-treated subgroup (see iter-3 analysis).
pembrolizumab x pdl1_tps interaction log-OR=+0.591, p=1.6e-7. Strongly positive: pembrolizumab benefit scales with PD-L1 expression.

PD-L1 high (TPS>=0.5, n=10749, 21.5% prevalence): pembrolizumab ORR 0.209 vs 0.156 off (diff +0.0536, p=7.7e-13). PD-L1 low (n=39251): pembro ORR 0.164 vs 0.166 (diff -0.0021, p=0.59). Confirms predictive role of PD-L1 for pembrolizumab.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps (worse performance status) is associated with lower objective_response.
stage_iv=1 patients have lower objective_response than non-stage_iv patients.
Patients with has_brain_mets=1 have lower objective_response than those without.
Patients with liver_mets=1 or bone_mets=1 have lower objective_response than those without.

analyses.result_summary: ecog_ps log-OR=-0.375 per 1-point increase, p=1.7e-93. Strong negative prognostic factor.
Stage IV (n=32365): ORR 0.154 vs 0.196 in non-stage IV. Difference -0.0425, p=1.1e-33.
Brain mets (n=12584): ORR 0.144 vs 0.177. Difference -0.0339, p=2.0e-18.

liver_mets (n=7546): ORR 0.173 vs 0.168, diff +0.0046, p=0.34. bone_mets (n=10067): ORR 0.173 vs 0.168, diff +0.0056, p=0.18. Neither distant-organ site is significantly prognostic in this cohort, contradicting the hypothesis (and contrasting with the strong brain-met effect).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l (inflammation marker) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: albumin_g_dl log-OR=+0.0907 per g/dL, p=1.4e-4. Higher albumin associated with higher response, as expected.
crp_mg_l log-OR=-0.00487 per mg/L, p=6.9e-4. Higher CRP associated with lower response, consistent with hypothesis.
weight_loss_pct_6mo log-OR=-0.0385 per percentage point, p=1.8e-32. Strong negative association.
nlr log-OR=-0.00502, p=0.384. Direction matches expectation but not significant in univariate test.
ldh_u_l log-OR=+0.00010, p=0.505. No association detected; direction is opposite to hypothesis but null result.
hemoglobin_g_dl log-OR=-0.0035, p=0.60. No association detected; direction opposite to hypothesis but null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Female patients (sex_female=1) have a higher objective_response rate than male patients.
Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response.
Patients with smoking_status='never' have a different objective_response rate than 'former' or 'current' smokers.
Black, Hispanic, Asian, or 'other' patients have a different objective_response rate than white patients.
Medicaid or uninsured patients have a lower objective_response rate than privately insured patients.

analyses.result_summary: Females: ORR 0.175 (n~24800), males: ORR 0.164. Difference +0.0116, chi-square p=5.9e-4. Modest but significant female advantage.
age_years log-OR=+0.00051, p=0.668. No association with response.
Never (n=7459) ORR 0.164; former (n=27567) 0.168 (log-OR vs never +0.034, p=0.34); current (n=14974) 0.172 (+0.061, p=0.11). No significant differences.

Reference white ORR 0.165. Black ORR 0.178 (log-OR +0.085, p=0.021, only one nominally significant). Asian 0.177 (+0.083, p=0.099). Hispanic 0.172 (+0.043, p=0.21). Other 0.185 (+0.138, p=0.09). Black-vs-white is the only significant racial difference and is small.
Private ORR 0.172. Medicaid ORR 0.161 (log-OR -0.077, p=0.040). Medicare 0.168 (-0.025, p=0.34). Uninsured 0.170 (-0.014, p=0.82). Medicaid vs private is the only significant insurance disparity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Major comorbidities (chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, copd, coronary_artery_disease, diabetes_mellitus) reduce objective_response.
Greater prior_lines_of_therapy is associated with lower objective_response.
prior_chemotherapy=1 is associated with lower objective_response than prior_chemotherapy=0.
autoimmune_disease=1 patients have a lower objective_response (since they may receive less aggressive immunotherapy or have altered immune milieu).

analyses.result_summary: Diabetes (p=0.76), hypertension (p=0.72), COPD (p=0.38), CKD (p=0.22), heart failure (p=0.89), CAD (p=0.051, ORR 0.162 vs 0.170), atrial fib (p=0.17), VTE (p=0.63), ILD (p=0.27), prior malignancy (p=0.45) -- none reaches significance. Comorbidity burden does not detectably affect ORR in this cohort.
prior_lines_of_therapy log-OR=-0.0123, p=0.244. Trend toward lower ORR with more prior lines but not significant.
Prior chemo (n=22409) ORR 0.166 vs 0.172 in chemo-naive. Difference -0.0062, p=0.067. Borderline trend, not significant.
Autoimmune disease (n=2397) ORR 0.175 vs 0.169. Difference +0.0066, p=0.42. Direction opposite to hypothesis; null result.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 23 germline SNPs (snp_rs* columns) is associated with objective_response after Bonferroni correction (p < 0.05/23 = 0.00217).
Beyond the matched-target biomarkers, additional somatic alterations (tp53_mutation, keap1_mutation, pik3ca_mutation, msi_high) have a main effect on objective_response.

analyses.code: for c in snp_cols: smf.logit(f'objective_response ~ {c}', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: 23 SNPs tested. Smallest p-values: rs7412 log-OR=+0.100, p=0.018; rs1050828 log-OR=-0.099, p=0.031. Bonferroni threshold 0.00217 -- no SNP survives correction. Hypothesis refuted.

analyses.code: chi2 for tp53, keap1, pik3ca, msi_high
analyses.result_summary: tp53_mutation (n=22479) p=0.88, keap1_mutation p=0.86, pik3ca_mutation (n=4013) ORR 0.179 vs 0.168, p=0.090, msi_high (n=487) p=0.74. None individually significant on main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: stk11_mutation reduces benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response (negative pembrolizumab x stk11_mutation interaction).
treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is larger in females (positive treatment_pembrolizumab x sex_female interaction).
treatment_osimertinib provides differential benefit in patients with has_brain_mets=1 (interaction term).

analyses.result_summary: STK11=0 (n=42549): pembro ORR 0.177 vs 0.162 (diff +0.0142, p=9.7e-5). STK11=1 (n=7451): pembro ORR 0.158 vs 0.173 (diff -0.0152, p=0.084). Interaction log-OR=-0.211, p=0.0018. Confirmatory: STK11 mutation flips the pembrolizumab effect.
treatment_pembrolizumab:sex_female log-OR=+0.146, p=0.0024. Pembrolizumab benefit appears greater in women than men.
treatment_osimertinib:has_brain_mets log-OR=+0.051, p=0.42. No detectable osimertinib brain-met-specific effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic regression for objective_response with adjustment for ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, albumin, weight loss, CRP, sex, age, PD-L1, TMB, STK11 and pembrolizumab interactions, the predictors that remain independently associated with response are: ecog_ps (negative), stage_iv (negative), has_brain_mets (negative), albumin_g_dl (positive), weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative), crp_mg_l (negative), sex_female (positive), and the pembrolizumab x pdl1_tps, pembrolizumab x tmb_high (positive), and pembrolizumab x stk11_mutation (negative) interactions.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + crp_mg_l + sex_female + pdl1_tps + tmb_high + treatment_pembrolizumab + treatment_pembrolizumab:tmb_high + treatment_pembrolizumab:pdl1_tps + treatment_pembrolizumab:stk11_mutation + stk11_mutation + age_years', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Independent predictors (multivariable logit): ecog_ps log-OR -0.382 (p<1e-90), stage_iv -0.302 (p<1e-30), has_brain_mets -0.257 (p<1e-17), albumin +0.092 (p=1.3e-4), weight_loss -0.039 (p<1e-30), crp -0.005 (p=4e-4), sex_female +0.081 (p=8e-4), pembrolizumab:tmb_high +0.174 (p=9.6e-4), pembrolizumab:pdl1_tps +0.595 (p<1e-6), pembrolizumab:stk11_mutation -0.204 (p=0.003). pdl1_tps main p=0.10, tmb_high main p=0.30, age and stk11 main NS. Confirms all hypothesized predictors except stk11/tmb/pdl1 main effects and age.
